data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: User experience design limits choices to simplify decision-making, enhancing ease of use.
data.technical_spec: Interface elements are categorized and limited to reduce cognitive load.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Designs prioritize clickability of commonly used elements to improve accessibility.
data.technical_spec: Interactive elements like play and pause buttons are large and easily navigable.

data.title: Gestalt Principles
data.description: Uses visual grouping to guide user focus and organize information intuitively.
data.technical_spec: Tabs, layout, and typography are designed to create visual hierarchy.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Group related elements to draw intuitive connections between interface components.
data.technical_spec: Related video suggestions and comments are spatially grouped.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Interface design accounts for human capacity to process limited amounts of information.
data.technical_spec: Menus and lists are broken into subcategories to enhance interaction efficiency.
